teachingHint: Your child is learning the /air/ sound (as in 'fair'). The three letters 'a', 'i', 'r' work together to make one sound: /air/. Help your child spot 'air' and blend: f-air → fair, ch-air → chair. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Was' doesn't sound like w-a-s. We say 'woz'. Learn it by sight.

story: The pair was not fair. She pulled his hair and let out air from the balloon!

story: You sat in the chair. It was not fair — the lair had no hair brushes!
